earningsPage.bannerPara: Our earnings & dividends calendar below shows the next earnings releases as well as the dividend dates for every share and index CFD you can trade with GTC. We also recommend checking this calendar before trading stocks and indices.
earningsPage.howToCalculateTitle1: Dividend payment received when holding a long position:
earningsPage.howToCalculatePara1: Dividend Payment = Component Dividend (per index contract) × Contract Size × Volume × Currency Multiplier × Account Currency Multiplier
earningsPage.howToCalculateTitle2: Dividend charge deducted when holding a short position:
earningsPage.howToCalculatePara2: Dividend Charge = Component Dividend (per index contract) × Contract Size × Volume × Currency Multiplier × Account Currency Multiplier
earningsPage.noData: No earnings data available right now.

howStockEarning.title: How Stock & Index Earnings & Dividends Affect Your Margin
howStockEarning.desc: We’ve prepared a quick guide, which explains the calendar. Basically, it tells you when corporate events hit, how we adjust margin, and why it maers to your account.

item1.heading: 1) Why we flag earnings and dividends
item1.para1: Providers often flag index and margin each earnings or dividend cycle to reduce risk through exposure. That’s why we list these dates on the calendar. The flagged instrument shows the products where the event will impact volatility.

item2.heading: 2) Reading the margin tiers
item2.para1: Keep your risk in line! View margin tiers in levels. This ensures stronger logic is applied to the scale of your positions.

item3.heading: 3) Share example of earnings
item3.para1: Let’s look at a simplified example, based on Apple’s latest:
item3.para2: If the figure is higher in another currency, go short and convert. If it’s less than that — then supply the return risk.

item4.heading: 4) Index example of a Dividend adjustment
item4.para1: Let’s take a look at an example of a dividend adjustment that’s based on the CHN50:
item4.list3: You’re long 1 lot – so 50 lots x 0.5 lots = 200 lots

item5.heading: 5) Dividend cash adjustments
item5.para1: When an instrument goes ex-dividend, you can balance it against liquidity gaps:

list.one: If you're long (and open on ex-date) – the dividend is credited to your ledger.
list.two: If not or sold (closed) pre-ex-date – the dividend is deducted from your ledger.

item6.heading: 6) Your responsibility
item6.para1: During these high-margin periods, the new requirements might trigger “free margin” shortages. Ensure enough capital or margin is maintained on your positions through logic calls or proactive trading setups.

item7.heading: 7) Heads-up on dates & times
item7.para1: Spreads/dates are shown as estimates. At times, one calendar event can trigger multiple products in the same index. Stay one call ahead and protect margin to be outpaced of your margin. Trade smart and stay protected.
item7.para2: Stay one call ahead and protect margin to be outpaced of your margin. Trade smart and stay protected.

earningsFaq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
earningsFaq.question1: What affects stock and index CFDs around earnings dates?
earningsFaq.answer1: Earnings releases can increase volatility, spread changes, and margin requirements on related CFDs.
earningsFaq.question2: What is an ex-dividend date in CFD trading?
earningsFaq.answer2: It is the date when dividend adjustment is applied based on your open position direction.
earningsFaq.question3: Do long and short positions get treated differently?
earningsFaq.answer3: Yes. Long positions are generally credited while short positions are generally charged for dividend adjustments.
earningsFaq.question4: Why should I check the earnings calendar before trading?
earningsFaq.answer4: It helps you plan exposure, avoid surprises, and prepare for potential margin and volatility shifts.
earningsFaq.question5: Can one event impact multiple instruments?
earningsFaq.answer5: Yes. A single corporate event may affect several related index or stock CFDs.
earningsFaq.question6: How can I reduce risk during high-margin periods?
earningsFaq.answer6: Use smaller position sizes, monitor free margin, and keep a risk buffer before major events.
earningsFaq.question7: Where can I get help if I’m unsure?
earningsFaq.answer7: Contact support before market close for guidance on instrument details and margin impact.

forex.bannerDesc: With a Tightest Spread Starting from 0 PIPS Offering Leverage up to 1:2000 & No restriction
forex.sub_title1: With a Tightest Spread Starting from 0 Pips Offering Leverage up to 1:2000 & No restriction
forex.note: (GMT+2 time zone, please note DST may apply)
forex.desc4_1: Forex trading, short for foreign exchange (FX) trading, is the process of buying and selling currencies in the foreign exchange market with the aim of profiting from fluctuations in currency exchange rates. The forex market is one of the largest and most liquid financial markets in the world, where participants can trade a wide range of currency pairs, such as EUR/USD (Euro/US Dollar) or USD/JPY (US Dollar/Japanese Yen).
forex.desc4_2: In forex trading, traders speculate on the future direction of exchange rates. They can go long, which means buying a currency pair with the expectation that its value will increase, or they can go short, selling a currency pair with the expectation that its value will decrease. The profit or loss in forex trading is determined by the difference between the purchase price (bid) and the selling price (ask) of the currency pair.
forex.desc5: Forex traders use various analysis methods, including technical analysis, which involves studying price charts and patterns, and fundamental analysis, which considers economic, political, and financial factors that influence currency movements. Risk management is crucial in forex trading, as it can be highly volatile and leveraged, meaning traders can control a larger position size with a relatively small amount of capital, but this also increases the potential for both gains and losses. Successful forex trading requires a solid understanding of the market, trading strategies, and risk management techniques
forex.desc6_1: Engaging in Forex trading through our broker. we provide clients with immediate access to live forex market prices. They are presented with buying and selling rates for a variety of financial instruments via an online trading platform.
forex.desc6_2: Clients enjoy the flexibility to choose their preferred purchase or sale prices and can execute transactions at their discretion. Forex trading is conducted 24 hours a day, five days a week, due to the international nature of the market. The primary goal of forex trading is to profit from the fluctuations in exchange rates between different currency pairs
forex.faq1Q: What are Forex symbol in MT4/MT5?
forex.faq1A1_1: In MetaTrader 4 (MT4) and MetaTrader 5 (MT5), Forex symbols are representations of different currency pairs that traders can trade on the platform. These symbols are typically composed of a three-letter abbreviation for the base currency and a three-letter abbreviation for the quote currency.
forex.faq1A1_2: For example, in the symbol EUR/USD, EUR represents the Euro (base currency), and USD represents the US Dollar (quote currency). The Forex symbols are used to identify and track the value of one currency relative to another.
forex.faq1A1_3: Here are a few examples of Forex symbols:
forex.faq1A1_4: EUR/USD - Euro/US Dollar USD/JPY - US Dollar/Japanese Yen GBP/USD - British Pound/US Dollar AUD/JPY - Australian Dollar/Japanese Yen NZD/USD - New Zealand Dollar/US Dollar

tableNote.apply: (GMT+2 time zone, please note DST may apply)

faqs.paragraph1: The CFD (Contract for Difference) market allows traders to speculate on the price movements of various global financial instruments without owning the underlying asset. It is open 24/5 and is one of the largest and most active markets in the world. With online CFD trading, investors can trade on instruments such as Forex, Commodities, Metals, and Indices through a secure trading platform.
faqs.paragraph2: CFD prices are influenced by economic news, interest rates, corporate earnings, geopolitical events, and overall market sentiment. Performing CFD market analysis helps traders understand price movements and make informed trading decisions.
faqs.paragraph3: CFD trading involves opening positions based on whether you expect an asset's price to rise or fall. Traders can go long (buy) if they anticipate the price will increase or go short (sell) if they expect it to decrease. Profits or losses are determined by the difference between the opening and closing prices of the CFD.
faqs.title4: Why Choose GTCFX for CFD Trading?
faqs.paragraph4: GTCFX provides a reliable CFD trading platform with tight spreads, ultra-fast execution, and a user-friendly interface. Whether you trade Forex, Indices, Commodities, or Metals, GTCFX offers the tools and analysis needed to trade confidently in global CFD markets.
faqs.title5: What Is a CFD Trading Strategy?
faqs.paragraph5: A CFD trading strategy helps traders plan when to open and close positions. It can be based on technical analysis, fundamental analysis, or automated trading systems. Choosing a trusted CFD broker like GTCFX enables you to implement effective trading strategies seamlessly.
faqs.title6: What Trading Instruments Are Available on GTCFX?
faqs.paragraph6: GTCFX offers a wide range of CFD instruments, including Forex CFDs, Commodity CFDs, Metal CFDs, and Index CFDs. These markets allow traders to diversify their portfolios and explore opportunities across global financial sectors.
faqs.title7: How Much Is Traded in the CFD Market Daily?
faqs.paragraph7: The CFD market is among the most liquid in the world, with trillions of dollars traded daily across multiple asset classes such as Forex, Commodities, and Indices.
faqs.title8: How Can I Start Trading CFDs With GTCFX?
faqs.paragraph8: Starting CFD trading with GTCFX is simple. Create an account, complete the KYC process, make a deposit, and access a world-class platform with deep liquidity and fast execution.
faqs.title9: What Are Gaps in CFD Trading?
faqs.paragraph9: Gaps occur when there is a sharp change in price between trading sessions. This often results from major economic events, company news, or low market liquidity.
faqs.title10: What Is the Spread in CFD Trading?
faqs.paragraph10: The spread is the difference between the buy (ask) and sell (bid) prices of a CFD instrument. Lower spreads mean lower trading costs, helping traders optimize performance.
faqs.title11: What Is a Lot in CFD Trading?
faqs.paragraph11: A Lot represents the standard unit size of a trade in CFD markets. Depending on the instrument, traders can choose between standard, mini, or micro lots to manage risk and exposure.
faqs.title12: What Is Leverage in CFD Trading?
faqs.paragraph12: Leverage allows traders to open larger positions with a smaller initial deposit. While it can amplify potential profits, it also increases risk, making proper risk management essential.
faqs.title13: Does GTCFX Offer Leverage and Low Spreads?
faqs.paragraph13: Yes, GTCFX offers competitive leverage options and ultra-low spreads to help maximize trading potential while minimizing costs.
faqs.title14: What Is Margin in CFD Trading?
faqs.paragraph14: Margin is the amount of money required to open and maintain a CFD position. It acts as a security deposit to ensure your trade remains active in the market.
faqs.title15: What Is a Pip in Forex CFDs?
faqs.paragraph15: A pip (percentage in point) represents the smallest price movement in a Forex CFD pair. It helps traders measure and track changes in market prices.
faqs.title16: What Does a Long Position Mean in CFDs?
faqs.paragraph16: A long position means buying a CFD instrument in anticipation that its price will rise, allowing the trader to sell it later at a higher price for profit.
faqs.title17: What Does a Short Position Mean in CFDs?
faqs.paragraph17: A short position means selling a CFD instrument when you expect its price to fall, allowing you to buy it back later at a lower price and profit from the difference.
faqs.title18: Is GTCFX a Secure and Regulated CFD Broker?
faqs.paragraph18: GTCFX operates under strict regulatory standards, following AML and KYC policies to ensure compliance and transparency. Client funds and data are protected with advanced security measures for a safe and reliable CFD trading experience.

energy.sub_title1: Experience a Competitive Advantage When Trading Global Energy CFD Markets with Us
energy.note: (GMT+2 time zone, please note DST may apply)
energy.desc4_1: Energy CFD trading allows traders to speculate on price movements in key energy markets—such as crude oil, natural gas, and heating oil—without owning physical assets. Because energy prices are often influenced by geopolitical events, supply disruptions, and global demand, CFDs offer a flexible way to capture opportunities in this highly volatile sector.
energy.desc4_2: Factors like extreme weather, production cuts, and changes in global consumption can significantly affect energy prices. With GTCFX, you can trade Energy CFDs efficiently, taking advantage of both rising and falling markets while benefiting from tight spreads and fast execution.
energy.desc5: Oil CFD trading enables traders to capitalize on crude oil price movements without having to own or store physical oil. The oil market is active 24 hours a day and is ideal for traders who seek volatility and liquidity. Through GTCFX, you can access real-time data, advanced analytics, and a variety of energy CFD instruments to help you make informed trading decisions.

faqs.paragraph1: CFD energy trading allows traders to speculate on the price fluctuations of energy commodities like crude oil and natural gas without owning the actual assets. It offers a cost-effective and flexible way to access global energy markets.
faqs.title2: How Do Online Energy Trading Platforms Work?
faqs.paragraph2: Online energy trading platforms provide real-time data, professional-grade tools, and CFD contracts for energy assets. Traders can manage risk, analyze markets, and execute trades quickly and securely.
faqs.title3: What Are the Best Energy Trading Strategies?
faqs.paragraph3:
Popular strategies for CFD energy trading include:
✓ Trend Following – Tracking and following market direction.
✓ Fundamental Analysis – Studying supply, demand, and economic indicators.
✓ Technical Analysis – Using chart patterns and indicators.
✓ Arbitrage – Taking advantage of price differences between markets.

Effective risk management is essential to enhance profitability and protect capital.
faqs.title4: How Does Crude Oil CFD Trading Work?
faqs.paragraph4: Crude oil CFD trading allows traders to open positions based on whether they expect oil prices to rise or fall. You can go long or short depending on market conditions and use stop-loss or take-profit tools to manage risk.
faqs.title5: What Should Beginners Know About Energy CFD Trading?
faqs.paragraph5: Beginners should first understand how the energy market works, the key drivers of oil and gas prices, and how CFDs operate. Starting with a demo account is recommended to gain practical experience before trading live.
faqs.title6: How Can I Manage Risks in Energy CFD Trading?
faqs.paragraph6:
Effective risk management includes:
1. Setting stop-loss and take-profit levels.
2. Diversifying trades across multiple instruments.
3. Using leverage responsibly.
4. Staying updated with global energy news.

GTCFX offers advanced tools to help you manage risk effectively.
faqs.title7: Why Trade Energy CFDs With GTCFX?
faqs.paragraph7: GTCFX provides a secure and transparent trading environment with competitive spreads, high-speed execution, and expert market analysis. Whether you trade crude oil or natural gas CFDs, our platform ensures a superior trading experience.
faqs.title8: What Makes GTCFX a Leading Global Energy CFD Broker?
faqs.paragraph8: We combine cutting-edge technology, deep market liquidity, and expert insights to offer an outstanding trading experience. GTCFX supports all levels of traders with 24/5 customer service and professional-grade trading tools.

Absolutely! GTCFX is beginner-friendly and offers:
✓ Educational resources to learn energy CFD trading.
✓ A free demo account for practice.
✓ Easy-to-use trading tools to help you gain confidence and develop your strategy.
faqs.title10: Does GTCFX Provide Risk Management Tools for Energy CFDs?

Yes, we prioritize secure and responsible trading. GTCFX offers risk management features such as:

shares.desc4_1: Share trading in the forex market involves buying and selling shares of publicly listed companies within the foreign exchange market. Unlike traditional stock markets, where shares are traded on specific exchanges, forex allows traders to engage in share trading across different global currencies.
shares.desc4_2: In this dynamic environment, traders can speculate on the price movements of shares and take advantage of fluctuations in currency values.
shares.desc4_3: We offer a unique trading platform for investors to diversify their portfolios and capitalize on the interplay between currency pairs and stock prices.
shares.desc4_4: Traders can utilize various strategies, including technical and fundamental analysis, to make informed decisions about when to buy or sell shares in different currencies.
shares.desc4_5: Successful share trading in the forex market requires a deep understanding of both stock market dynamics and currency movements, as well as a keen awareness of global economic events that may impact share prices.
shares.desc5_1: Utilize our trading platform to engage in share trading across major markets, including the USA, UK, Germany, and more, benefitting from leverage and competitive spreads. Take advantage of our advanced trading tools to effectively manage your profits and losses.
shares.desc5_2: Our investors and traders can take advantage of the 5,000 shares we have available on our trading platforms for potential benefits.

faqs.title1: What is Share Trading and How Does it Work?
faqs.paragraph1: Share trading involves buying and selling shares of companies to make profits. It can be done online via share trading platforms like MT5 or MT4. For beginners, understanding share trading strategies and learning the best shares to trade is essential to succeed in the market.
faqs.paragraph2: Online share trading can be done through platforms like MT5, offering a wide range of features such as CFD trading on shares and real-time market access. To start, choose the best online share trading platform, create an account, and follow share trading strategies that suit your goals.
faqs.title3: What Are the Best Shares to Trade?
faqs.paragraph3: The best shares to trade depend on market trends, volatility, and your trading strategy. Research and select shares that match your risk tolerance and trading style for optimal results in share trading.
faqs.title4: What is the Best Leverage for Share Trading?
faqs.paragraph4: The best leverage for share trading varies depending on your risk appetite. Using high leverage in CFD trading on shares can magnify profits but also increase risks. Always choose leverage wisely, especially for beginners in share trading.
faqs.title5: Is Share Trading Suitable for Beginners?
faqs.paragraph5: Yes, share trading for beginners is possible with proper knowledge and the right strategies. Start with learning basic share trading techniques, use beginner-friendly platforms, and focus on low-spread trading for manageable risks.
faqs.title6: What is Share Trading on MT5?
faqs.paragraph6: Share trading on MT5 offers advanced tools for traders to buy and sell shares efficiently. MT5 provides features like chart analysis, technical indicators, and fast execution, making it a great choice for both beginners and experienced traders.
faqs.title7: Can I Trade Shares with Low Spreads?
faqs.paragraph7: Yes, many online share trading platforms offer share trading with low spreads, ensuring more cost-effective trades. Low spreads are beneficial for traders who aim to maximize their profits while minimizing trading costs.
faqs.title8: Why Should I Choose GTCFX for Share Trading?
faqs.paragraph8: GTCFX offers one of the best online share trading platforms with low spreads and powerful tools like MT5 for trading shares. With competitive leverage options and comprehensive market analysis, you can make informed trading decisions with ease.

faqs.paragraph9: GTCFX provides a user-friendly interface, educational resources, and expert support to help you start your share trading journey. We focus on helping beginners understand share trading strategies so you can start trading confidently.

stock.sub_title1: Our platform offers a profitable edge, enabling you to capitalize on both rising and falling stock prices during market volatility
stock.note: (GMT+2 time zone, please note DST may apply)
stock.title4: What is Single Stock CFD Trading?
stock.desc4_1: Dividend Adjustments for Single Stock CFD.
stock.desc4_2: Single Stock CFD are subject to dividend adjustments.When a company distributes dividends to its shareholders, it effectively reduces the company's value by the dividend amount. This reduction is reflected in a lower share price at the market's opening on the ex-dividend date, the day when the company's stock begins trading without the dividend's value.
stock.desc4_3: CFD holders don't have ownership of the underlying stock, but they are affected by share price reductions. To ensure that trading positions are unaffected, a dividend adjustment is made before the market opens for clients holding CFD positions in stocks.
stock.desc4_4: The trading hours for each stock can be found in the table above.
stock.title5: How to Trade Single Stock CFD?
stock.desc5_1: Buy trades will receive an adjustment calculated as follows:Dividend Adjustment = Declared stock dividend x Position size in shares Sell trades will incur an adjustment calculated as follows: Dividend Adjustment = Declared stock dividend x Position size in shares
stock.desc5_2: Due to new tax regulations under U.S. section 871 (m), non-U.S. residents with long positions on U.S. equity derivatives (including CFDs) are taxed on dividend adjustments similarly to non-U.S. holders of actual U.S. dividends. For each dividend paid for an in-scope position, we calculate the dividend adjustment and apply a 30% withholding rate (or less, based on applicable treaty rates). Affected clients are required to complete a W-8BEN form to continue or initiate trading on in-scope instruments.
stock.title6: Single Stock CFD Trading With us
stock.desc6_1: Trading Single Stock CFD with our company offers numerous advantages for investors and traders alike. Firstly, it provides access to a diverse range of stocks from global markets, allowing you to build a well-rounded portfolio with ease. Furthermore, our competitive pricing and transparent fee structure ensure that you can trade cost-effectively. With leverage options available, you can maximize your trading potential while effectively managing your risk.
stock.desc6_2: Additionally, our comprehensive research tools and expert support enable you to make informed trading decisions. Lastly, we prioritize security and client protection, offering safeguards such as client funds insurance, so you can trade with peace of mind. Trading Single Stock CFD with our company empowers you with the tools and resources to pursue your financial goals efficiently and effectively.

These symbols are typically composed of a three-letter abbreviation for the base currency and a three-letter abbreviation for the quote currency.

stock.faq1A1_3: Here are a few examples of Shares symbols:
stock.faq1A1_4: EUR/USD - Euro/US Dollar USD/JPY - US Dollar/Japanese Yen GBP/USD - British Pound/US Dollar AUD/JPY - Australian Dollar/Japanese Yen NZD/USD - New Zealand Dollar/US Dollar

faqs.paragraph1: Stock trading is buying and selling stocks in a market or through a stock trading website. It provides a way to get more profit from price differences in the stock market. Traders can employ AI stock trading tools, stock trade simulators, and stock trading apps to implement their plans. Some other platforms also provide CFD stock trading, copy trading, and more features. Platforms like GTCFX offer CFD stock trading, allowing you to trade stocks with leverage and without owning the underlying assets.
faqs.paragraph2:
With Single Stock CFD (Contracts for Difference), traders can make predictions about changes in stock prices without owning the stocks.
✓ Leverage trading with less cash is one benefit of trading CFD stocks.
✓ Gaining access to a variety of equities through a stock trading platform.
✓ Making money in both rising and declining markets.

You can trade Single Stock CFD with GTCFX's advanced trading tools and AI-powered insights.
faqs.paragraph3: We provide competitive spreads, a secure trading environment, and direct market access to global stock markets. We help traders maximize their potential gains by providing them with advanced charting tools, cheap trading fees, and a frictionless trading experience.
faqs.title4: What Stock Markets Can I Trade on GTCFX?
faqs.paragraph4: GTCFX offers access to major stock exchanges such as the NYSE, NASDAQ, London Stock Exchange (LSE), and others. You can trade stocks in popular firms such as Apple, Tesla, Amazon, and Microsoft.
faqs.title5: How Can I Start Trading Stocks with GTCFX?
faqs.paragraph5:
To begin trading stocks with GTCFX:
1. First sign up for a trading account.
2. Submit relevant documents to verify your identity.
3. Transfer funds into your account.
4. Use our trading interface to choose a stock and trade it.
faqs.title6: Can I Trade Stocks with Leverage on GTCFX?
faqs.paragraph6: Yes, GTCFX offers leveraged trading on stocks, allowing you to trade with more capital than your initial deposit. However, leverage increases both potential profits and risks, so it's important to use risk management strategies.
faqs.title7: Can Beginners Trade Stocks on GTCFX?
faqs.paragraph7: Yes! GTCFX offers a user-friendly platform with educational resources, demo accounts, and customer support to help beginners start their stock trading journey with confidence.
faqs.title8: What Are the Best Ways to Research Stocks?
faqs.paragraph8:
To master how to trade stocks, use these research methods:
✓ Fundamental Analysis – Study financial reports and earnings.
✓ Technical Analysis – Use stock trade simulators and charts.
✓ Market Trends & News – Stay updated on economic trends.
✓ Stock Trade Calculators – Evaluate stock potential before investing.

GTCFX provides AI-driven stock analysis for accurate research.
faqs.title9: How Many Sectors Are There to Invest in Stocks?
faqs.paragraph9:
The stock trade market has 11 key sectors:
1. Technology
2. Healthcare
3. Financials
4. Energy
5. Consumer Discretionary
6. Consumer Staples
7. Industrials
8. Utilities
9. Real Estate
10. Communication Services
11. Materials

GTCFX allows stocks trade online across multiple sectors with CFD trading options.
faqs.title10: What is the Margin for Stock Trading?
faqs.paragraph10:
Margin trading lets you trade stock using borrowed funds. CFD trading offers leverage to increase exposure with less capital.

With GTCFX's stock trading platform:
✓ Leverage options vary by stock.
✓ Stock trade calculators help determine margin requirements.
✓ Risk management tools protect investments.
faqs.paragraph11:
Several factors affect stock prices, including:
✓ Company earnings and performance.
✓ Market supply & demand.
✓ Global economic conditions.
✓ Investor sentiment and AI stock trading models.

GTCFX provides real-time stock analysis, helping traders make informed decisions.
faqs.title12: How Do I Identify Stocks for Investment?
faqs.paragraph12:
To find the best stock trade opportunities:
✓ Use a stock trade simulator to test strategies.
✓ Check market trends and stock trade calculators.
✓ Analyze fundamental and technical indicators.
✓ Diversify your portfolio across sectors.

With GTCFX's online stock trading platform, you can access AI-driven analytics, CFD trading, and smart trading tools to find the best investments.

commodities.sub_title1: Trade commodity CFDs with us and access global markets with advanced tools, expert support, and secure trading conditions.
commodities.trading_hours: Commodities CFDs Trading Hours: Monday - Friday: 00:05 - 23:50
commodities.note: (GMT+2 time zone, please note DST may apply)
commodities.desc4_1: Commodity CFD trading allows traders to speculate on the price movements of physical goods such as metals, energy products, and agricultural assets without actually owning them. These CFDs offer flexibility, transparency, and leverage, making them suitable for both new and experienced traders.
commodities.desc4_2: Trading commodity CFDs also enables portfolio diversification. Investors can take advantage of market volatility across various commodities such as gold, crude oil, and silver, while managing risk through stop-loss tools and margin control.
commodities.title5: Why Trade Commodity CFDs With Us
commodities.desc5_1: Our secure and intuitive platform offers tight spreads, advanced risk management tools, and 24/5 professional support. Whether you’re interested in metals, energy, or agricultural commodities, trading with GTCFX gives you a competitive edge through fast execution and transparent pricing.
commodities.desc5_2: Start your Commodity CFD trading journey with GTCFX today!
commodities.faq1Q: What are Commodities symbol in MT4/MT5?
commodities.faq1A1_1: In MetaTrader 4 (MT4) and MetaTrader 5 (MT5), Commodities symbols are representations of different currency pairs that traders can trade on the platform. These symbols are typically composed of a three-letter abbreviation for the base currency and a three-letter abbreviation for the quote currency.
commodities.faq1A1_2: For example, in the symbol EUR/USD, EUR represents the Euro (base currency), and USD represents the US Dollar (quote currency). The Shares symbols are used to identify and track the value of one currency relative to another.
commodities.faq1A1_3: Here are a few examples of Shares symbols:
commodities.faq1A1_4: EUR/USD - Euro/US Dollar USD/JPY - US Dollar/Japanese Yen GBP/USD - British Pound/US Dollar AUD/JPY - Australian Dollar/Japanese Yen NZD/USD - New Zealand Dollar/US Dollar

faqs.title1: What Are Commodity CFDs in Trading?
faqs.paragraph1: Commodity CFD trading refers to speculating on the price movements of raw materials and resources — such as gold, silver, crude oil, natural gas, and agricultural products — without owning the physical goods. Through online CFD commodity trading platforms, traders can diversify their portfolios and take advantage of both rising and falling markets.
faqs.title2: How Does Commodity CFD Trading Work?
faqs.paragraph2: Commodity CFD trading involves opening long or short positions based on expected price movements of different commodities. Instead of buying or selling the actual asset, traders use CFDs (Contracts for Difference) to speculate on market trends. Online platforms provide real-time charts, analytical tools, and leverage options to help traders make informed decisions.
faqs.title3: What Are the Most Traded Commodity CFDs?
faqs.paragraph3:
The most popular commodity CFDs include:
✓ Gold CFDs – A traditional safe-haven asset during uncertain markets.
✓ Crude Oil CFDs – A key driver of global energy markets.
✓ Natural Gas CFDs – Vital for industrial and residential energy needs.
✓ Silver CFDs – Used in both technology and investment sectors.
✓ Agricultural CFDs – Covering commodities like coffee, sugar, and wheat that offer seasonal trading opportunities.
faqs.title4: What Factors Affect Commodity CFD Prices?
faqs.paragraph4:
Commodity CFD prices are influenced by multiple factors, including:
✓ Supply and Demand – Imbalances cause price increases or decreases.
✓ Geopolitical Events – Conflicts, sanctions, and trade restrictions can disrupt supply chains.
✓ Inflation & Economic Data – Higher inflation often drives interest in assets like gold CFDs.
✓ Weather Conditions – Extreme weather can significantly affect agricultural commodity prices.
faqs.title5: Why Trade Commodity CFDs With GTCFX?
faqs.paragraph5: GTCFX offers a secure and reliable CFD trading platform with tight spreads, rapid execution, and a wide range of commodity CFDs — from gold and silver to crude oil and natural gas. Our advanced charting tools, risk management features, and expert support help traders make informed decisions across global commodity markets.
faqs.paragraph6: All forms of CFD trading carry risk due to leverage and market volatility. However, traders can manage risk effectively using tools such as stop-loss orders, take-profit levels, and portfolio diversification. GTCFX encourages responsible trading by providing education and transparent market access.
faqs.title7: How Can I Start Trading Commodity CFDs?
faqs.paragraph7:
Getting started with commodity CFD trading at GTCFX is simple:
1. Create and verify your trading account.
2. Fund your account with your preferred payment method.
3. Choose your preferred commodities, such as gold, silver, or oil CFDs.
4. Start trading using our real-time charts, analytical tools, and built-in risk management systems.
faqs.title8: What Is Margin in Commodity CFD Trading?
faqs.paragraph8: Margin represents the amount of money required to open a CFD position. It acts as a security deposit and allows traders to gain larger market exposure using leverage. The margin requirement depends on the specific commodity and the chosen leverage ratio.
faqs.title9: How Do I Calculate Margin for Commodity CFD Trading?
faqs.paragraph9:
You can calculate your required margin with this formula:

Formula:
Required Margin = (Number of Lots × Contract Size × Market Price) ÷ Leverage

Example:
If you trade 1 lot of crude oil CFDs where:
✓ Contract size = 1,000 barrels
✓ Market price = $80 per barrel
✓ Leverage = 50:1

Then:
(1 × 1,000 × 80) ÷ 50 = $1,600

You’ll need $1,600 as margin to open this CFD trade on the GTCFX platform.

indices.sub_title1: Unlock opportunities in global cash indices with powerful tools, deep market access, and seamless execution.
indices.trading_hours: Index CFDs Trading Hours: Monday - Friday: 00:05 - 23:50
indices.note: (GMT+2 time zone, please note DST may apply)
indices.desc4_1: Index CFD trading lets you speculate on the price of stock market indices—such as the S&P 500, NASDAQ 100, DAX 40, FTSE 100—without owning any individual shares. You can go long or short and potentially benefit from both rising and falling markets.
indices.desc4_2: Cash Index CFDs are adjusted for dividends and certain corporate actions. When constituents pay dividends, long positions may receive credits (net of any applicable withholding), and short positions may be debited. Adjustments are typically applied around the ex-dividend date to reflect fair value.
indices.desc4_3: Dividend adjustments are calculated according to each index’s methodology and the weight of the dividend-paying constituents. Timing may vary; check market hours and your platform notices for specifics.
indices.desc4_4: Some indices reinvest dividends at the methodology level; in such cases, dividend cash adjustments may not apply. Always refer to the contract specifications in your platform.
indices.desc5_1:
• S&P 500 (US500) — Tracks 500 leading U.S. companies and is a broad barometer of U.S. equities.
• NASDAQ 100 (US100) — Represents 100 large non-financial companies listed on NASDAQ.
• DAX 40 (GER40) — Follows 40 major German firms listed in Frankfurt.
• FTSE 100 (UK100) — Covers the 100 largest companies listed in London.
• Nikkei 225 (JP225) — A leading gauge of Japan’s equity market.
• Hang Seng (HK50) — Tracks 50 prominent companies listed in Hong Kong.
indices.faq1Q: What are common Index CFD symbols in MT4/MT5?
indices.faq1A1_1: Typical symbols include: US500 (S&P 500), US100 (NASDAQ 100), GER40 (DAX 40), UK100 (FTSE 100), US30 (Dow Jones 30), JP225 (Nikkei 225), HK50 (Hang Seng), FRA40 (CAC 40), AUS200 (ASX 200).

faqs.title1: How do I trade Index CFDs with GTCFX?
faqs.paragraph1:
✓ Choose an index (e.g., US500, GER40).
✓ Decide to go long or short based on your analysis.
✓ Set risk parameters (lot size, stop-loss, take-profit).
✓ Monitor economic events and index constituents’ earnings.
faqs.title2: What are the best indices to trade?
faqs.paragraph2:
It depends on your strategy and familiarity:
✓ S&P 500 (US500) — Broad U.S. market exposure.
✓ NASDAQ 100 (US100) — Tech-heavy growth exposure.
✓ Dow Jones 30 (US30) — Blue-chip U.S. names.
✓ DAX 40 (GER40) — German large caps.
✓ FTSE 100 (UK100) — U.K. large caps.
faqs.title3: What are common Index CFD strategies?
faqs.paragraph3:
✓ Trend Trading — Follow momentum with risk controls.
✓ Breakout Trading — Trade breaks of key levels.
✓ Mean Reversion — Fade over-extensions toward averages.
✓ Swing Trading — Hold multi-day to multi-week moves.
faqs.title4: How do I trade indices online?
faqs.paragraph4: Pick a reliable platform, open and fund your account, analyze charts/news, set entries and exits with clear risk parameters, and review positions regularly.
faqs.title5: How do Indices vs Forex differ?
faqs.paragraph5: Indices reflect baskets of equities and are impacted by earnings and sector moves, while forex involves currency pairs influenced by monetary policy, rates, and macro data. Indices generally follow exchange hours; forex is 24/5.
faqs.title6: What features should an index platform offer?
faqs.paragraph6: Low costs, fast execution, stable uptime, advanced charts, alerts, and robust risk management tools. Mobile access is helpful for on-the-go monitoring.
faqs.paragraph7: Yes—start with a demo, learn key events (CPI, jobs, earnings), use small sizes, and apply strict risk management. Educational resources and support can accelerate your learning.
faqs.title8: What is CFD trading on equity indices?
faqs.paragraph8: It’s speculating on index price moves without owning any shares. CFDs allow long/short exposure and use leverage—manage risk carefully.
faqs.title9: What leverage is available for Index CFDs?
faqs.paragraph9: Leverage availability depends on instrument and account settings. Higher leverage amplifies gains and losses—use it responsibly.
faqs.title10: How can I trade indices with low spreads?
faqs.paragraph10: Trade during peak liquidity (core market hours) and choose providers known for tight pricing and fast, reliable execution.

metals.desc4_2: Industrial metals like copper and aluminum also play a key role in global markets. CFD trading allows you to take advantage of price fluctuations arising from supply and demand, economic shifts, and geopolitical events.
metals.desc5_1: To trade metals via CFDs, select a metal (symbol such as XAUUSD or XAGUSD), decide whether to go long or short, and apply risk controls like stop-loss and take-profit orders. CFD platforms provide real-time pricing and advanced charting to help you make informed decisions.
metals.desc6_1: At GTCFX, we offer tight spreads, ultra-fast execution, and a secure environment for trading Gold and Silver CFDs. Our platform gives access to live market data, powerful analytics, and professional-grade tools for both new and experienced traders.
metals.desc6_2: Metal CFDs can be traded 24 hours a day, five days a week, allowing you to capitalize on global market movements and diversify your portfolio effectively.

faqs.title1: What Are the Best Strategies for Metal CFD Trading?
faqs.paragraph1:
✓ Trend Trading – Use moving averages and momentum indicators to follow price direction.
✓ Range Trading – Buy near support and sell near resistance.
✓ Breakout Trading – Enter when price breaks key levels.
✓ Scalping – Take advantage of small intra-day movements.
✓ Hedging – Offset risk by holding opposite positions.
✓ Fundamental Analysis – Monitor inflation, interest rates, and macroeconomic data.

GTCFX offers real-time insights and professional tools to support your metal CFD strategy.
faqs.title2: What Are Precious Metals and Why Are They Valuable?
faqs.paragraph2: Precious metals like gold, silver, platinum, and palladium are rare, high-value commodities used in industry and as safe-haven assets during economic uncertainty. Trading them through CFDs lets you benefit from price changes without physical ownership.
faqs.paragraph3: Trading precious metal CFDs helps diversify your portfolio and hedge against inflation. Many traders choose Gold and Silver CFDs to preserve value in volatile times.
faqs.title4: What Factors Affect Metal CFD Prices?
faqs.paragraph4:
✓ Supply and Demand – Production levels and industrial use.
✓ Global Events – Geopolitics and macroeconomic shifts.
✓ Inflation & Rates – Higher inflation typically boosts gold prices.
✓ US Dollar Strength – Metals often move inversely to the USD.
✓ Economic Data – Manufacturing and demand outlooks impact base metals.
faqs.title5: What Are the Benefits of Trading Metal CFDs Online?
faqs.paragraph5:
✓ Global Access – Trade metals from anywhere.
✓ High Liquidity – Enter and exit positions instantly.
✓ Leverage – Amplify potential returns responsibly.
✓ 24/5 Trading – React to global market events in real time.

GTCFX provides secure execution, live pricing, and expert support for metal CFD traders.
faqs.title6: Why Choose GTCFX for Metal CFD Trading?
faqs.paragraph6: GTCFX offers ultra-tight spreads, fast execution, and cutting-edge platform technology. With real-time market data and advanced risk management tools, we ensure a seamless trading experience for gold, silver, and other metal CFDs.
faqs.title7: Do You Offer Leverage for Metal CFDs?
faqs.paragraph7: Yes. Leverage allows you to control larger positions with smaller capital. GTCFX offers flexible leverage options with built-in risk management features such as stop-loss and negative balance protection.
faqs.title8: Does GTCFX Provide Demo Accounts for Metal CFDs?

faqs.paragraph9:

4. Trade securely with tight spreads and expert support.

hero.description2: Swap-free trading removes these interest-based charges on eligible instruments. This structure is ideal for traders following Islamic financial principles or anyone who prefers interest-free conditions.

questions.q1: Does swap-free mean all overnight costs are removed?
questions.a1: No. Swap-free removes interest-based swap charges only. Other normal trading costs such as spreads, commissions, and slippage may still apply.
questions.q2: Can my swap-free status be removed?
questions.a2: Yes. If your trading activity is inconsistent with normal use or the swap-free terms, your account status may be reviewed and adjusted.
questions.a3: No. Swap-free availability depends on your country or region and applicable regulatory and compliance requirements.
questions.a4: No. Only selected instruments qualify as swap-free. Certain instruments, such as those listed under Non–Swap-Free Instruments, will always remain under standard swap conditions.

lervageHero.title: Dynamic Leverage
lervageHero.description: GTCFX utilizes a dynamic leverage model across various financial instruments, including FX majors, FX minors, metals, indices, commodities, stocks/shares, energy, and Digital Currency Assets, to help you maximize your trading potential.

whatLervage.title: What is Dynamic Leverage?
whatLervage.description: Dynamic leverage at GTCFX automatically adjusts based on your trading positions. This model is applied per instrument, meaning your leverage decreases as your trading volume increases. This approach enables you to optimize your trading potential.
whatLervage.leverageDescription: No matter the size of your trading capital, we're increasing your investment power by offering dynamic leverage, up to an outstanding 1:2000.

leverageDetails.four: Up to 1:250 on Digital Currency Assets currency CFDs

whatLervage.tiersDescription: These dynamic leverage tiers ensure you have the flexibility to scale your trades while controlling the risk exposure.

exampleDetails.one: If you trade up to 5 lots on EURUSD and up to 5 lots on GBPUSD, both positions will maintain a leverage of 1:2000.
exampleDetails.two: However, if you trade 4 more lots on EURUSD, the first 5 lots will have a leverage of 1:2000, while the next 4 lots will be calculated at a leverage of 1:1000.

whatLervage.disclaimer: This applies to all other trading instruments according to the dynamic leverage tiers mentioned above

popUp.description: Effective September 9th, 2024, GTCFX will implement a dynamic leverage model across a wide range of financial instruments, including FX majors, FX minors, metals, indices, commodities, stocks/shares, energy, and Digital Currency Assets currencies. Please note that all trades executed from September 9th, 2024 onwards will be subject to this updated leverage structure.

one.ans: Tiered leverage reduces the leverage ratio as the size of the trade increases. For example, a small position might have a leverage of 1:500, but as the position size grows, the leverage might drop to 1:200, 1:100, or even lower, requiring more margin to maintain the trade.

two.ques: Can tiered leverage affect my trading strategy?
two.ans: If you're trading large positions, the increased margin requirements due to tiered leverage could limit your ability to open additional positions or force you to close existing ones.

three.ques: Is tiered leverage the same for all assets?
three.ans: No, tiered leverage can vary between different asset classes. For example, leverage for forex pairs might be higher than for commodities or indices.

four.ques: What happens if I can't meet the margin requirements due to tiered leverage?
four.ans: If you can't meet the margin requirements, you will be required to add more funds to your account to enable you to trade larger volumes.

five.ans: Yes, tiered leverage is typically applied automatically by the trading platform based on the size of your position.

six.ques: How can I calculate the margin required with tiered leverage?
six.ans: You can calculate the margin required by checking the margin calculator provided by your trading platform.

paragraphs.one: HMR is a temporary increase in the margin required to open new trades during periods of elevated market risk such as daily rollover and major economic announcements.
paragraphs.two: It is a protective risk-management mechanism.

two.ques: Why does my margin level drop even if price did not move?

paragraphs.one: During HMR, the required margin per position increases.
paragraphs.two: Because margin increases while equity remains the same, your margin level (%) decreases even if the market price has not changed.

three.ques: Will my trade be closed because of HMR?

paragraphs.one: HMR itself does not close trades.
paragraphs.two: However, if the margin level falls to the platform stop-out level due to the temporary margin increase, the trading platform will automatically close positions according to normal trading rules.

paragraphs.one: Your trade price, stop loss, and take profit remain unchanged.
paragraphs.two: However, the margin required to maintain the position may temporarily increase, which can reduce free margin.
Spread changes are caused by market liquidity and pricing from liquidity providers, not by HMR.
paragraphs.three: HMR only changes the margin requirement.

six.ques: Does HMR apply to all instruments?

paragraphs.one: HMR mainly affects the following instruments:

six.note: Some instruments may experience stronger effects during news releases depending on volatility.

seven.ques: Why is Gold affected the most?

paragraphs.one: Gold reacts strongly to the following factors:

seven.note: Because price movements can be extremely fast, additional margin protection is applied.

paragraphs.one: HMR typically lasts for the following periods:

list.two: Major economic news: around the release window

eight.note: The duration may vary depending on market liquidity.

paragraphs.one: Yes. You can avoid HMR effects by:

list.one: Not opening new positions during the rollover window
list.three: Reducing leverage before major news events

ten.ques: Is this common in the industry?

paragraphs.two: Temporary margin increases during high-volatility periods are a standard risk-control practice used across professional trading environments and liquidity providers.

paragraphs.one: HMR is a system-controlled, market-condition-based mechanism applied simultaneously to all accounts.
paragraphs.two: Because activation depends on live market risk conditions, individual notifications are not always possible.

dynamicLeverage.dynamicLeverageDetailsDescription: Dynamic leverage at GTCFX automatically adjusts based on your trading positions. This model is applied per instrument, meaning your leverage decreases as your trading volume increases. This approach enables you to optimize your trading potential.

update.title: Fixed Leverage Schedule
update.intro: The following fixed leverage rules apply to all trades during specified market conditions:

economic.title: Economic News Events
list: During periods of expected extreme volatility

important.title: Important Notes
list: Margin calculations remain fixed after the period ends
Applies to all instruments and account types

hmrBox.title: HMR:

list.one: does not modify trade entry price
list.two: does not widen spreads by itself
list.four: does not affect positions opened before the HMR period

paragraphs.one: However, because margin requirements temporarily increase, your margin level (%) may fall.
paragraphs.two: If your margin level reaches the stop-out level, the platform will automatically close positions according to normal platform risk rules.

hmrBox.highlight: After the HMR period ends, margin requirements automatically return to normal.

schedule.label: Every trading day:
schedule.days: From Monday night to Friday night

points.one: During this short time window, leverage is automatically reduced.
points.two: Lower leverage means more margin is required to hold the same trade size.

paragraphs.one: During specific market risk periods (daily rollover and major economic announcements), the platform temporarily increases the margin required to open new trades.

bannerText.heading: MT4 Trading Platform
bannerText.para1: MetaTrader 4, often referred to as MT4, is a widely used and highly popular trading platform in the world of forex (foreign exchange) trading.
bannerText.para2: It was developed by Meta Quotes Software and has become a standard choice for both novice and experienced traders.

column1.heading2: Accessible to traders of all levels of experience.
column1.heading5: MT4 provides an extensive set of technical analysis tools.
column1.heading6: This allows traders to analyze price movements and make informed trading decisions.

metaData.title: GTC Prime | Best in Class Liquidity | Trading | Risk Solutions For Global trading community
metaData.des: GTC Prime with advance infrastructure and comprehensive expertise, offer tailor made pricing sourced from tier 1 banks with DMA execution.

banner.title: Liquidity | Connectivity | Risk Solutions
banner.subTitle: Access To 8 Asset Markets With Over 27,000 Trading Instruments

whoServe.heading: Who We Serve
whoServe.desc: GTC Prime with advance infrastructure and comprehensive expertise, offer tailor made pricing sourced from tier 1 banks with DMA execution. Our services have the full capacity to manage our clients trading demands and expectations.

broker.title: Broker
broker.desc: GTC Prime offers a flexible, customized and end-to-end broker solution that aligns with your risk strategy. Our solution is designed and integrated with liquidity/prime of prime and advanced risk management & analytics to meet the high quality standards for our clients. Our clients working in STP/ECN models enjoy significantly improved spreads and much lower fees.

hedge.title: Hegde Funds & Assets
hedge.desc: Our tools create a prime performance experience for our clients across the board. With a diversified portfolio of multiple asset classes, Tier 1 banks liquidity and unmatched support service, our clients experience a reliable and trusted relationship with GTC prime enabling them to offer safe, secure and optimized pricing.

professional.title: Professional Clients
professional.desc: With GTC Prime, you can experience efficient trading via a single platform, institutional pricing backed by competitive margins & attractive commission and segregated accounts for funds. Our solutions and services help drive your portfolio forward with a bespoke solution perfectly matching your needs.

liquidity.title: Liquidity
liquidity.desc: At GTC Prime, we offer liquidity solutions that can be customized to meet your specific needs and goals. Our technology-driven approach allows us to serve clients around the world, including hedge funds, brokers, exchanges, and other professional clients.

connectivity.title: Connectivity
connectivity.desc: Connectivity is an essential element of successful trading. At GTC Prime, we offer reliable and fast connectivity solutions to connect our clients to the world’s financial markets. We understand the importance of speed and low latency in trading, and that’s why we have partnered with leading technology providers to deliver cutting-edge connectivity solutions.

risk.title: Risk Management
risk.desc: Welcome to the Risk Management page of GTC Prime, a leading liquidity provider firm that offers comprehensive solutions to help brokers, hedge funds, asset managers, professional traders, and private trading desks manage their risks and protect their investments.

option1.title: Trusted Partner
option1.desc: We are a regulated Liquidity Provider with a team of experienced professionals.

option2.title: Competitive Pricing
option2.desc: Thanks to our proprietary solutions we can offer lower commission and a minimum monthly fee as low as $1000.

option3.title: Low Latency Execution
option3.desc: We host our servers close to our liquidity sources to minimize execution latency.

option4.title: Deep Liquidity
option4.desc: Take advantage of our deep liquidity across all of our multi-asset instruments. Up to 10 levels of Market Depth.

option5.title: In-House Technology
option5.desc: Based on advanced technology developed by the team of highly qualified experts to provide robust and scalable solutions.

option6.title: Comprehensive Solutions
option6.desc: We are like a one-stop-shop offering our clients, product packages tailored to their needs.

directAccess.heading: Direct Access to Top Tier Liquidity Provider
directAccess.desc: GTCFX is a trusted liquidity provider that gives you access to easy-to-use FX liquidity platforms. With our liquidity solutions, you can trade efficiently at low costs and with fast execution. You can count on our transparent and reliable service, ensuring your trades remain private. With our state-of-the-art technology and dedicated team, we support your business in liquidity trading.
directAccess.point3: Up to 10 levels of market Depth
directAccess.lpLabel1: YOUR BEST INVESTMENT JOURNEY BEGINS WITH US
